channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: Wildlife killed, reefs damaged in ‘active’ Gulf of Mexico oil spill
item.description: Authorities say the oil is seeping from three sources, including a vessel that has not yet been identified.

item.title: India lowers fuel taxes, says rumours of lockdown ‘completely false’
item.description: India, the world's third biggest crude importer, announces measures amid shortages created by the Iran war.

item.title: War on Iran price crisis not just about energy, says analyst
item.description: Abdulla Banndar Al-Etaibi, says the disruption of fertiliser supplies due to the war on Iran will push food prices up.

item.title: Video: Children’s toys found in rubble of US-Israeli strike in Iran
item.description: Rescue teams from the IRCS have been searching the rubble of residential buildings hit in US-Israeli attacks on Iran.

item.title: Iran emergency workers search for survivors after deadly US-Israel attacks
item.description: Latest victims include six people killed in Qom as Iran announces grim milestone of nearly 2,000 war dead.

item.title: How extensive is Russia’s military aid to Iran?
item.description: Russia has long exchanged weapons with Iran and is likely offering satellite support, but lacks the will to do much more

item.title: Israeli opposition leader rails against ‘multi-front war without strategy’
item.description: Yair Lapid says military 'is stretched to the limit and beyond' as Israeli forces wage attacks on Iran and Lebanon.

item.title: Iran bans sports teams from travelling to ‘hostile’ countries amid war
item.description: Sports Ministry says ban for 'security' of athletes before a game between Iranian and Emirati clubs in Saudi Arabia.

item.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 28 of attacks?
item.description: Trump has delayed attacks on Iran's energy facilities by 10 days, claiming talks are going well - though Iran disagrees.

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz is not just an oil chokepoint
item.description: It is the single point of failure of globalised production.
